text: Add backend-owned runtime state endpoints for autonomy metadata
description: Expose .bowman/runtime state through backend APIs so the frontend can observe autonomy/session/runtime state without inferring it from localStorage or transient socket-only state. This should become the read path for durable autonomy state.

reflections.context: Expose durable autonomy/watchdog runtime state through backend APIs so the frontend can observe project-local runtime truth instead of inferring it from transient sockets or localStorage.
reflections.outcome: Added backend project router endpoints to read and update watchdog runtime state under .bowman/runtime/watchdog-state.json, and used them from the frontend store to hydrate and persist preferred runtime state.
reflections.effectiveness: The endpoint slice was the right size. It moved ownership in the right direction without forcing a full autonomy framework redesign first.
reflections.lesson: Autonomy architecture benefits from a stable file format plus thin backend endpoints before adding more UI or scheduler behavior.
reflections.pattern: This follows the same pattern as aims/phases/meta: file-backed project state becomes much easier to reason about once the backend provides a canonical access path.

status.comment: Added backend-owned project runtime state endpoints so the frontend can read and update durable watchdog runtime state without relying on browser-local inference.
